The function deactivates the context before exporting it. Use [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/gss_import_sec_context(_:_:_:)] to restore the token to a context and reactivate it. Only one instance of a given context may be active at a time.

# gss_export_sec_context(_:_:_:)

**Function**

Transfers a security context to another process.

## Declaration

```swift
func gss_export_sec_context(_ minor_status: UnsafeMutablePointer<OM_uint32>, _ context_handle: UnsafeMutablePointer<gss_ctx_id_t?>, _ interprocess_token: gss_buffer_t?) -> OM_uint32
```

## Parameters

- **minor_status**: A pointer to the secondary status result that provides additional information in case of failure.
- **context_handle**: The context to export.
- **interprocess_token**: A buffer the function fills with a token corresponding to the context. Release the buffer storage with a call to [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/gss_release_buffer(_:_:)] when you are done with it.

## Return Value

A status code set to [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/GSS_S_COMPLETE] on success. See [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/function-status] for a description of other status outputs.

## Discussion

The function deactivates the context before exporting it. Use [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/gss_import_sec_context(_:_:_:)] to restore the token to a context and reactivate it. Only one instance of a given context may be active at a time.

## Import and Export

- **gss_import_sec_context(_:_:_:)**: Imports a security context from another process.
